raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The request to help with the section centrally includes improving how the modeling contribution and takeaway are framed at a section level.","R1.2":"The section contains a chain from architecture and training choices to results and conclusions, so checking coherence is an expected responsibility.","R1.3":"The reported WER numbers and ablation result are used to support claims about performance and augmentation, making evidence-claim alignment central.","R1.5":"Because the delegation targets an entire section, section-level narrative order and arc are central expected concerns.","R1.6":"A broad request to help with a section most directly loads responsibility onto sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.","RX.1":"Any assistance should preserve uncertainty around tuning, prior comparison, and causal interpretation rather than presenting unsupported certainty.","RX.2":"The artifact repeatedly hedges comparative and causal claims, so avoiding stronger claims than the evidence supports is always active.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within helping with the section rather than executing unrelated research, experiments, or literature review.","RX.4":"Care is needed to avoid introducing misleading performance or novelty claims that could distort downstream scholarly use.","RX.5":"Any projected responsibilities should remain traceable to the provided section and its reported model, datasets, metrics, and ablation."}}

rationale.R1.1: The request to help with the section centrally includes improving how the modeling contribution and takeaway are framed at a section level.
rationale.R1.2: The section contains a chain from architecture and training choices to results and conclusions, so checking coherence is an expected responsibility.
rationale.R1.3: The reported WER numbers and ablation result are used to support claims about performance and augmentation, making evidence-claim alignment central.
rationale.R1.5: Because the delegation targets an entire section, section-level narrative order and arc are central expected concerns.
rationale.R1.6: A broad request to help with a section most directly loads responsibility onto sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any assistance should preserve uncertainty around tuning, prior comparison, and causal interpretation rather than presenting unsupported certainty.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact repeatedly hedges comparative and causal claims, so avoiding stronger claims than the evidence supports is always active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within helping with the section rather than executing unrelated research, experiments, or literature review.
rationale.RX.4: Care is needed to avoid introducing misleading performance or novelty claims that could distort downstream scholarly use.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected responsibilities should remain traceable to the provided section and its reported model, datasets, metrics, and ablation.
